items.title: Random thought I had watching Vijay Singh practice putting with no one around at TPC Wisconsin:
items.author: Jim Owczarski
items.body_excerpt:
Random thought I had watching Vijay Singh practice putting with no one around at TPC Wisconsin:

Golf is an individual, transient sport. The players play around the world, for themselves. Some win majors. That's big.

What I always found interesting is they don't really like, celebrate it them.

items.title: Filed story before they closed the MSG press room: rare for me.
items.author: Dan Devine
items.body_excerpt:
Filed story before they closed the MSG press room: rare for me.

Exited MSG while it was still light out: a first!

Hit shuffle. First up; Blur, "Beetlebum."

Walked through NYC on a gorgeous day covering the Knicks in the Finals, listening to my late best friend's favorite band. Life can be great.

items.title: Mike Brown's 'I don't run plays for my guys' Princeton Motion Offense
items.author: MutaKingPrime
items.body_excerpt: Mike Brown after a scorching 3-0 start to preseason this year said that he did not really run any plays for his team. On the surface you think that's crazy. But Mike Brown's offense is a read-and-react, motion offense largely built on Princeton principles. Sometimes the Knicks…
